Physician Printed Name (first Name, last name, middle Initial, suffix)
Encompass Health Rehabilitation Hospital of Henderson
REFERRING PHYSICIAN: Jayson L. Bautista APRN
ADMITTING PHYSICIAN MD Minh H. Nguyen MD
HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS: This is an 82-year-old female with history of stroke who
presents with bilateral lower extremity weakness and gait disturbance. The patient also
reportedly had acute emesis and diarrhea. The patient's previous CVA with right-sided
deficits as well as chronic low back pain. The patient was seen by her home health and
felt that she had declined and recommended acute rehab,
PAST MEDICAL HISTORY: Significant for previous CVA with residual
SOCIAL HEALTH HISTORY: Nonsmoker, nonalcoholic drinker.
MEDICATIONS: Aspirin 81 mg daily, atorvastatin 10 mg daily, Lovenox 40 mg subcutaneously
daily, gabapentin 600 mg daily, Keppra 250 mg daily, levothyroxine 88 mcg daily,
pantoprazole 40 mg daily, and sertraline 50 mg daily.
REVIEW OF SYSTEMS: A 12-point system review was performed, otherwise negative except
VITAL SIGNS: Temperature 98.3, heart rate 72, respirations 18, and BP 132/61,
HEENT: Normocephalic and atraumatic. Pupils equal and reactive. Oropharynx without
Encompass Health Rehabilitation Hospital of Henderson
NECK: Negative JVD or carotid bruits.
LUNGS: Clear to auscultation. Negative rales, wheezes or rhonchi.
ABDOMEN: Obese, soft, and nontender. Negative hepatosplenomegaly.
NEUROMUSCULAR EXAMINATION: Cranial nerves II-XII are grossly intact with no focal
deficits. Manual muscle testing of the upper extremities are 5/5 and lower extremity
2. Bilateral lower extremity weakness with status post ground level fall on 02/01.
3. Previous CVA in 2015 with some residual weakness.
The patient has impairment in bed mobility, transfers, unsteady gait and balance, easy
fatigability and poor endurance and pain and overall loss of functional independence.
Because of above medical problems and functional issues, it is my opinion the patient
should be in acute rehab unit rather than a lower level of setting of care. Therefore,
she will need multidisciplinary approach, three hours a day of therapy for five days a
week, close medical supervision for 24 hours, and 24-hour rehab nursing care.
There are no clinical significant differences between the patient's current status and
information described in a preadmission screening document.
There are NO safety or special Considerations that would likely preclude immediate
implementation of an intensive rehabilitation program (3 hours per day, 5 days a week)
or substantially influenced the plan of care
Considering all the information above, it is my best judgment that this patient requires
intensive rehabilitation therapy as described above in an inpatient hospital environment
due to complexity of nursing, medical management and rehabilitation needs. Furthermore,
this patient can reasonably be expected to participate in and benefit from an inpatient
stay with an interdisciplinary team approach to the delivery of rehabilitation care under
the direction and supervision of a rehabilitation physician.
1. Impairment with ambulation and mobility: Continue physical therapy.
2. Impairment of activities of daily living: Continue occupational therapy.
5. Check laboratory data on a regular basis.
6. Will treat the patient as the clinical course unfolds.
Encompass Health Rehabilitation Hospital of Henderson
REHABILITATION GOALS: Rehabilitation goals are to maximize patient's independence with
ADLs, self care, mobility, communication skills and patient's medical status. Specific
goals are to be established following completion of the assessment period. Goals include:
1. The patient is to be at a modified independent level with ambulation and mobility, and
also with activities of daily living.
2. The patient is to be medically stable.
REHABILITATION POTENTIAL/PROGNOSIS: Good, per the above stated goals.

Treatment Summary/Progress : Pt tolerated tx well today. Ther act, and galt training completed per grid. Treatment
emphasized on gait mechanics. Pt responded well to increasing her endurance, however still amb with antaigic galt
pattern. Pt still has difficulty with L hip pain, heat pack was provided this session. Pt will cont to benefit from skilled PT
Intervention to improve functional mobility. Session ended with pt in bed, bed alarm activated, c/l and tray table within
reach, and all needs met. RN notified of pt's return.
